sourceMovie.title: Life of the Party
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Life of the Party works as a comedy movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: Hook
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Life of the Party clicked for you, Hook (1991) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Life of the Party?
faq.answer: Try Hook, Bridget Jones's Diary, American Reunion—each shares DNA with Life of the Party while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Life of the Party?
faq.answer: Life of the Party (2018) is best described as Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Life of the Party worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
